title: `last_sql_use_alloc` is not correct for result chunk
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
drop table if exists t;
create table t(id int primary key, col int);
insert into t values (1, 1);
select * from t where id = 1;
select @@last_sql_use_alloc;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

`last_sql_use_alloc` is 1

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

`last_sql_use_alloc` is 0.

It indeed reused the chunk in connection, but it didn't change the value of `last_sql_use_alloc`.

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(id INT PRIMARY KEY, col VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
key_bug_features: `last_sql_use_alloc` value not updated after chunk reuse
